title: A Secondary Path-Decoupled Active Noise Control Algorithm Based on Deep Learning
summary: This paper introduces a novel Secondary Path-Decoupled Active Noise Control (SPD-ANC) algorithm that leverages deep learning to address the performance degradation and filter divergence issues in conventional ANC systems caused by nonlinearities in the secondary path. The proposed method utilizes two time-domain Convolutional Recurrent Networks (CRNs) to model the nonlinear secondary path and its inverse. This allow…

key_references.title: Active noise control: A tutorial review
key_references.reason: Provides a foundational tutorial on active noise control systems.

key_references.title: Nonlinear FXLMS algorithm for active noise control systems with saturation nonlinearity
key_references.reason: Discusses nonlinear FXLMS algorithms for ANC, a relevant baseline for handling secondary path nonlinearities.

key_references.title: Deep ANC: A deep learning approach to active noise control
key_references.reason: Introduces a deep learning approach to ANC, serving as a comparison point for deep learning methods.

key_references.title: A time-domain convolutional recurrent network for packet loss concealment
key_references.reason: Introduces the time-domain convolutional recurrent network (CRN) architecture used in the proposed method.

key_references.title: Performance comparison of the FXLMS, non-linear FXLMS and leaky FXLMS algorithms in nonlinear active control applications
key_references.reason: Compares traditional and nonlinear FXLMS algorithms, providing context for performance evaluation.

title: Deep learning-assisted active noise control in a time-varying environment
venue: Journal of Mechanical Science and Technology
summary: This paper introduces a novel deep learning-assisted method for active noise control (ANC) in time-varying environments. The core innovation lies in a two-stage deep neural network (DNN) designed to estimate the secondary path impulse response in real-time, adapting to changing acoustic conditions. This addresses a critical limitation of traditional ANC systems, which struggle when the environment changes. The propo…

key_references.title: Process of Silencing Sound Oscillations
key_references.reason: This reference provides the foundational patent for active noise control.

key_references.title: Active noise control: A tutorial review
key_references.reason: This is a comprehensive tutorial review of active noise control, covering algorithms and implementations.

key_references.title: Active Noise Control Systems: Algorithms and DSP Implementations
key_references.reason: This book is cited multiple times for fundamental concepts and algorithms in ANC, including the FxLMS algorithm and secondary path modeling.

key_references.title: Online secondary path modeling for active sound quality control systems
key_references.reason: This paper discusses online secondary path modeling, a relevant technique for time-varying environments.

key_references.title: Deep learning
key_references.reason: This is a foundational paper on deep learning, providing context for the use of DNNs in the proposed method.

key_references.title: Scikit-learn: machine learning in Python
key_references.reason: This reference details the Scikit-learn library used for implementing the DNN models.

title: Real-time implementation and explainable AI analysis of delayless CNN-based selective fixed-filter active noise control
summary: This paper presents a real-time implementation and explainable AI analysis of a delayless Convolutional Neural Network (CNN)-based Selective Fixed-Filter Active Noise Control (SFANC) method. The approach leverages CNNs to automatically learn parameters for selecting appropriate pre-trained control filters based on noise characteristics. Theoretical analysis frames ANC as a Markov process to justify the CNN-based SFA…

key_references.title: Selective fixed-filter active noise control based on frequency response matching in headphones
key_references.reason: This paper introduces the selective fixed-filter ANC (SFANC) concept, which is foundational to the presented work.

key_references.title: Feedforward selective fixed-filter active noise control: Algorithm and implementation
key_references.reason: This work provides the algorithmic basis for SFANC, which the current paper builds upon.

key_references.title: Selective fixed-filter active noise control based on convolutional neural network
key_references.reason: This paper first proposed using CNNs for SFANC, directly preceding the current work.

key_references.title: LayerCAM: Exploring hierarchical class activation maps for localization
key_references.reason: This paper introduces LayerCAM, the explainable AI technique used in the current study.

key_references.title: ShuffleNet V2: Practical guidelines for efficient CNN architecture design
key_references.reason: The paper's CNN architecture is based on ShuffleNet V2, making it a relevant methodological reference.

key_references.title: Transferable latent of cnn-based selective fixed-filter active noise control
key_references.reason: This related work by the same authors investigates the transferability of CNN-based SFANC, a key aspect also addressed in the current paper.

title: Enhancing active noise control of road noise using deep neural network to update secondary path estimate in real time
summary: This paper introduces a novel deep learning-based approach to enhance active noise control (ANC) performance in vehicles by enabling real-time updates of the secondary path estimate. The dynamic nature of vehicle environments causes significant variations in the secondary path, degrading ANC effectiveness. The authors identified key spatial conditions (vertical, horizontal, roll, yaw) influencing these variations an…

key_references.title: Advances in active noise control: A survey, with emphasis on recent nonlinear techniques
key_references.reason: Provides a broad overview of active noise control techniques, setting the foundation for the problem addressed in the paper.

key_references.title: Active noise control systems: algorithms and DSP implementations
key_references.reason: Discusses fundamental algorithms like FxLMS, which are central to ANC systems and the context of this work.

key_references.title: A review of virtual sensing algorithms for active noise control
key_references.reason: Introduces virtual sensing, a related concept to real-time path estimation for ANC in dynamic environments.

key_references.title: Deep learning-based active noise control: A deep learning approach to active noise control
key_references.reason: Represents prior work applying deep learning to ANC, though not specifically for real-time secondary path estimation in vehicles.

key_references.title: Deep learning-assisted active noise control in a time-varying environment
key_references.reason: Explores deep learning for ANC in time-varying environments, similar to the dynamic conditions addressed in this paper.

title: Transferable Selective Virtual Sensing Active Noise Control Technique Based on Metric Learning
venue: IEEE/ACM Transactions on Audio, Speech, and Language Processing
summary: This paper introduces a novel Transferable Selective Virtual Sensing (VS) Active Noise Control (ANC) technique that leverages metric learning to enhance the transferability of Convolutional Neural Network (CNN)-based VS systems. The core innovation is a pre-trained CNN feature extraction module that can be applied to new ANC systems without retraining, enabling it to handle unseen noise types. This approach signific…

key_references.title: Selective virtual sensing technique for multi-channel feed-forward active noise control systems
key_references.reason: Introduces the selective virtual sensing technique which is a precursor to the proposed method.

key_references.title: Cognitive virtual sensing technique for feedforward active noise control
key_references.reason: Presents a related CNN-based approach for virtual sensing that the proposed method builds upon and aims to improve.

key_references.title: Transferable latent of cnn-based selective fixed-filter active noise control
key_references.reason: This work demonstrates the transferability of CNN models in a related ANC context, providing motivation and a foundation for the proposed transferable approach.

key_references.title: Distance metric learning: A comprehensive survey
key_references.reason: Provides a foundational overview of metric learning, a core technology used in the proposed method.

key_references.title: Metric learning: A survey
key_references.reason: Offers a comprehensive survey of metric learning techniques, relevant to understanding the theoretical underpinnings of the proposed approach.

key_references.title: Deep residual learning for image recognition
key_references.reason: Introduces residual learning, which is incorporated into the CNN architecture used in the proposed method.

title: Spatially selective active noise control systems
summary: This paper introduces a novel multi-channel active noise control (ANC) system designed for spatial selectivity. Unlike traditional ANC systems that aim to minimize all sound, this proposed system selectively reduces noise from undesired directions while preserving desired sound sources. The core innovation lies in imposing a spatial constraint, derived from the Frost algorithm, onto the cost function of a hybrid ANC…

key_references.title: Combined feedforward-feedback noise reduction schemes for open-fitting hearing aids
key_references.reason: This paper explores combined feedforward-feedback noise reduction for hearing aids, which is a precursor to hybrid ANC systems.

key_references.title: An algorithm for linearly constrained adaptive array processing
key_references.reason: This foundational work introduces the Frost algorithm, which is central to the spatial constraint used in the proposed method.

key_references.title: Design and implementation of an active noise control headphone with directional hear-through capability
key_references.reason: This paper presents a related approach for ANC headphones with directional hear-through, offering a point of comparison.

key_references.title: Integrated active noise control and noise reduction in hearing aids
key_references.reason: This work integrates ANC and noise reduction in hearing aids, touching upon similar concepts of selective noise control.

key_references.title: Adaptive Filter Theory
key_references.reason: This is a standard textbook on adaptive filters, providing theoretical background for the adaptive algorithms used.

title: Cognitive Virtual Sensing Technique for Feedforward Active Noise Control
summary: This paper introduces a cognitive virtual sensing (VS) technique to improve the performance of active noise control (ANC) systems, particularly when noise characteristics or primary paths change over time. Unlike conventional VS methods that struggle with such variations, the proposed cognitive VS technique uses a lightweight classifier to analyze both reference and monitoring microphone signals. This allows the sys…

key_references.title: A hybrid SFANC-FxNLMS algorithm for active noise control based on deep learning
key_references.reason: This paper introduces a hybrid SFANC-FxNLMS algorithm using a lightweight M6-res network for noise classification, which is a precursor to the cognitive VS technique proposed here.

key_references.title: Selective virtual sensing technique for multi-channel feedforward active noise control systems
key_references.reason: This work is cited as a previous selective VS technique that the proposed cognitive VS technique improves upon, particularly in handling primary path variations.

key_references.title: Robust performance of virtual sensing methods for active noise control
key_references.reason: This paper discusses the robustness of virtual sensing methods, providing context for the challenges addressed by the cognitive VS technique.

key_references.title: MobileNets: Efficient convolutional neural networks for mobile vision applications
key_references.reason: This paper introduces depthwise separable convolution, a key component adopted and modified for the lightweight classifier in the proposed cognitive VS technique.

key_references.title: Feedforward selective fixed-filter active noise control: Algorithm and implementation
key_references.reason: This paper details the selective fixed-filter active noise control (SFANC) system, which is a foundational concept that the cognitive VS technique builds upon and enhances.

title: Co-initialization of Control Filter and Secondary Path via Meta-Learning for Active Noise Control
summary: This paper introduces a novel Model-Agnostic Meta-Learning (MAML) based co-initialization strategy for Feedforward Filtered-x Least Mean Squares (FxLMS) active noise control (ANC) systems. The primary goal is to improve the initial performance and adaptation speed of ANC systems when the acoustic environment changes. Unlike previous methods that might initialize only the control filter or rely on standard FxLMS with…

key_references.title: Online secondary path modeling in active noise control without auxiliary noise
key_references.reason: This paper explores online secondary path modeling, a core component addressed by the proposed initialization method.

key_references.title: Model-agnostic meta-learning for fast adaptation of deep networks
key_references.reason: This is the foundational paper for Model-Agnostic Meta-Learning (MAML), the core meta-learning technique used in this work.

key_references.title: Fast adaptive active noise control based on modified model-agnostic meta-learning algorithm
key_references.reason: This prior work also applies MAML to ANC, but focuses on initializing only the control filter, whereas this paper co-initializes both.

key_references.title: On comparison of online secondary path modeling methods with auxiliary noise
key_references.reason: This paper provides a foundational comparison of online secondary path modeling methods, which is the context for the proposed initialization.

key_references.title: Acoustic path database for ANC in-ear headphone development
key_references.reason: This paper provides the measured acoustic paths used for training and evaluation in the proposed method.

title: An Integration Development of Traditional Algorithm and Neural Network for Active Noise Cancellation
venue: 2022 IEEE International Workshop on Machine Learning for Signal Processing (MLSP)
summary: This paper proposes an integrated active noise cancellation (ANC) system that combines a traditional adaptive IIR filter with a deep learning model to address nonlinear distortions that limit the performance of conventional ANC methods. The adaptive IIR filter first handles the linear component of the noise, and a subsequent neural network, specifically a Dual-Signal Transformation LSTM (DTLN) followed by a TasNet-l…

key_references.title: Deep ANC: A Deep Learning Approach to Active Noise Control
key_references.reason: This paper proposes a deep learning method for nonlinear ANC, similar to the neural network component of the current work.

key_references.title: Development of Equation-Error Adaptive IIR-Filter-Based Active Noise Control System
key_references.reason: This reference introduces the Equation-Error (EE) based adaptive IIR filter, which is used as the traditional component in the proposed integrated system.

key_references.title: Dual-signal transformation LSTM network for real-time noise suppression
key_references.reason: This paper describes the Dual-Signal Transformation LSTM (DTLN) network, which is adapted for the frequency-domain processing in the proposed system.

key_references.title: Conv-TasNet: Surpassing Ideal Time–Frequency Magnitude Masking for Speech Separation
key_references.reason: This paper introduces the TasNet structure, which is adapted for the time-domain processing in the proposed system.

key_references.title: The Filtered-X LMS Algorithm
key_references.reason: The Filtered-X LMS (FXLMS) algorithm is a widely used traditional method for ANC and serves as a baseline for comparison.
